title: Floral Crunchy Peruvian Wild Mushrooms Stew
description: A vibrant, energizing stews that combines wild Peruvian mushrooms, aji amarillo, and crunchy quinoa in a pressurecooked, paleofriendly dish. The floral notes of lime and cilantro balance the earthy mushrooms and give it a refreshing finish.

ingredients.name: wild mushrooms (chanterelles, morels, or mixed wild variety)
ingredients.notes: clean, trim stems, slice or leave whole for texture

ingredients.name: quinoa
ingredients.notes: rinse thoroughly and cook to a slightly crunchy texture

ingredients.name: fresh cilantro, chopped
ingredients.notes: for final garnish and floral brightness

instructions: Set your pressure cooker to sauté mode and heat 1 tbsp olive oil.
Add minced garlic and grated ginger; cook until fragrant (~1 minute).
Add the aji amarillo paste and stir to combine, cooking for another 30 seconds to bloom the flavors.
Add sliced wild mushrooms to the pot, sautéing until they release moisture and start to brown, about 3 minutes.
Stir in the rinsed quinoa, ensuring it coats evenly with the aromatics.
Pour in the vegetable broth, then give the mixture a good stir to combine.
Seal the pressure cooker and cook on high pressure for 10 minutes.
Quick‑release the pressure, then open the lid carefully.
Stir in the lime juice and chopped cilantro, tasting and adjusting salt and pepper as needed.
Transfer the stew to serving bowls, sprinkle toasted pumpkin seeds on top for a crunchy finish, and serve hot.
